meaning: he comes to them (referring to the disbelievers receiving a new reminder)
do you all come? (questioning the people who denied the message)
The All-Hearing (one of Allah's names, subject of the sentence)
The All-Knowing (one of Allah's names, describing His complete and perfect knowledge of everything)
so let him bring to us
before them (referring to the people of the destroyed towns)
men (as the ones being sent by Allah)
they were (referring to the messengers mentioned earlier)
those who last forever (as a description in this sentence)
your (plural) reminder/mention (referring to what Allah has sent down in the book)
you all use your minds (addressing the disbelievers in the ayah)
and how many (connects to previous verses about destroyed towns)
we broke/destroyed (main verb of the sentence)
you all were made to live in luxury
in it (referring to what they were given luxuries in)
that (feminine, pointing to something far away)
their claim (referring to those who denied the truth)
players/those who act without purpose (describing how Allah did NOT create the heavens and earth)
that (connects 'if We had intended' to 'We take')
near, in the presence of (as an adverb)
and then it crushes it completely
and whoever (connects this phrase to the previous description of beings in the heavens)
they spread out/bring back to life
if (introducing an impossible situation in 'if there were gods besides Allah')
in/within both of them (referring to heavens and earth)
they both would surely have become broken (referring to the heavens and earth)
they describe (referring to those who attribute partners to Allah)
by/with the speech (they don't precede Him)
he knows (what is before them and after them)
their hands (referring to those being discussed in the ayah)
so that/therefore (referring to the punishment mentioned next)
like that/similarly (referring to the punishment mentioned)
and we made/placed (connecting to previous statement)
mountains (because they are firmly fixed in the earth)
it (the earth) sways (lest it should sway with them)
they move smoothly (referring to the sun and moon in their orbits)
and to us (showing where you will be returned)
you all seek to rush me
and they speak/say (asking about the promise)
if (in a hypothetical situation that didn't happen)
when (referring to the time when the disbelievers will not be able to ward off the Fire)
rather (introduces what happens to them instead)
and then it makes them speechless (them refers to disbelievers)
and so it surrounded (connecting to what happened to previous messengers)
they mocked (with emphasis on group action)
from them (referring to those who mocked)
with it (referring to what they were mocking)
he guards/protects you all (referring to the disbelievers being addressed)
but rather (contrasts with what was said before about who protects them)
their Lord (referring to those who turn away from remembrance in this ayah)
help (as the object being acted upon)
these ones/these people (pointing to something nearby)
the lifespan (that has become long for them)
they see (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
a thing (that will not be wronged)
and they (who fear their Lord in private)
for/to her/it (referring to idols they worship)
or (in questions: 'are you one of those who play?')
he created them (referring to the heavens and earth)
and I (Ibrahim, connecting to his previous statement)
who (asking about the person who did something to our gods)
indeed he (referring to the one who did this to our gods)
this (referring to the idols they are discussing)
O Abraham! (being called by his people)
he did it (referring to breaking the idols)
their chief/biggest one (referring to the idols)
indeed you all (addressing those who accused themselves of wrongdoing)
then, next, afterwards (shows sequence of events)
they were turned upside down (referring to the idolaters in the story of Ibrahim)
they speak (referring to the idols who cannot speak)
Do you (all) then worship? (question to the disbelievers about their idols)
you all serve/worship (addressing the polytheists who worship idols)
burn him intensely! (command to a group)
and help/support! (command to a group of people)
coolness (as a command to the fire)
Ibrahim (Abraham) - as the object being addressed
indeed they (referring to the people of Lot's town who did evil deeds)
those who break away from obedience
the people (who are being saved from)
of the people (referring to the owners of the sheep mentioned earlier in the ayah)
and so we made her understand it
a piece of armor (that protects you)
in order to protect you all
your (all of you) might (protecting you from harm)
We blessed (in this land that is mentioned)
ones who know deeply (plural, in object form)
his Lord (the one being called out to)
O my Lord! (calling upon the Lord)
leave me (Prophet Zakariya asking Allah not to leave him)
in desire, eagerly (describing how they call upon Allah)
for us (they were humble to us)
Our spirit (that We breathed into her)
when (shows something that will happen in the future)
rather/but rather (correcting the previous statement about being in heedlessness)
indeed you all (emphasizes that you all will be fuel for hellfire)
they arrived at it (referring to Hell)
a heavy breathing sound (of the people in Hell)
those who - relative pronoun for plural
those who are made distant/kept away
they hear (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
their souls (referring to those who will dwell forever in paradise)
those who last forever (plural, in regular case)
we will return/bring it back (with emphasis)
my servants (who will inherit the earth)
this one (points to something specific in this ayah)
they turned themselves away (from the truth)
far away (referring to something not specified yet)
perhaps he/it (referring to what was mentioned)
The Most Merciful (as a divine name of Allah)
The One whose help is sought
